chunks.title: JavaScript Variables, Data Types & Operators
chunks.text: In this video, we will learn about JavaScript variables, data types, and operators in detail.

chunks.title: JavaScript Variables, Data Types & Operators
chunks.text: Let keyword declares a block-scoped variable that can be reassigned.

chunks.title: JavaScript Variables, Data Types & Operators
chunks.text: Const keyword declares a block-scoped variable that cannot be reassigned after initialization.

chunks.title: JavaScript Variables, Data Types & Operators
chunks.text: Block scope means the variable is only accessible within the curly braces where it is defined.

chunks.title: JavaScript Variables, Data Types & Operators
chunks.text: JavaScript is a dynamically typed language, meaning you don't need to specify data types.

chunks.title: JavaScript Variables, Data Types & Operators
chunks.text: The typeof operator returns the data type of a variable.

chunks.title: JavaScript Variables, Data Types & Operators
chunks.text: Arithmetic operators include addition, subtraction, multiplication, division, and modulus.

chunks.title: JavaScript Variables, Data Types & Operators
chunks.text: The modulus operator returns the remainder of a division.

chunks.title: JavaScript Variables, Data Types & Operators
chunks.text: String concatenation is done using the plus operator to join two strings together.

chunks.title: JavaScript Variables, Data Types & Operators
chunks.text: Template literals use backticks and allow you to embed expressions using dollar sign curly braces.

chunks.title: JavaScript Variables, Data Types & Operators
chunks.text: Template literals are much cleaner than string concatenation for building dynamic strings.

chunks.title: JavaScript Variables, Data Types & Operators
chunks.text: Comparison operators include equal to, not equal to, greater than, less than.

chunks.title: JavaScript Variables, Data Types & Operators
chunks.text: The double equals checks value equality, while triple equals checks both value and type.

chunks.title: JavaScript Variables, Data Types & Operators
chunks.text: Always use triple equals for comparison in JavaScript to avoid unexpected type coercion.

chunks.title: JavaScript Variables, Data Types & Operators
chunks.text: Logical operators include AND, OR, and NOT, written as double ampersand, double pipe, and exclamation mark.

chunks.title: JavaScript Variables, Data Types & Operators
chunks.text: AND returns true only if both operands are true. OR returns true if at least one is true.

chunks.title: JavaScript Variables, Data Types & Operators
chunks.text: NOT reverses the boolean value, turning true to false and false to true.

chunks.title: JavaScript Variables, Data Types & Operators
chunks.text: The assignment operator is the equals sign. There are also compound assignment operators.

chunks.title: JavaScript Variables, Data Types & Operators
chunks.text: Plus equals adds and assigns, minus equals subtracts and assigns, and so on.

chunks.title: JavaScript Variables, Data Types & Operators
chunks.text: The increment operator plus plus increases a value by one. Decrement minus minus decreases by one.

chunks.title: JavaScript Variables, Data Types & Operators
chunks.text: Type conversion can be done using Number(), String(), and Boolean() functions.

chunks.title: JavaScript Variables, Data Types & Operators
chunks.text: JavaScript also performs automatic type coercion in certain situations which can be confusing.

chunks.title: JavaScript Variables, Data Types & Operators
chunks.text: Understanding these fundamentals is crucial before moving to more advanced JavaScript topics.

text: In this video, we will learn about JavaScript variables, data types, and operators in detail. Let keyword declares a block-scoped variable that can be reassigned. Const keyword declares a block-scoped variable that cannot be reassigned after initialization. Block scope means the variable is only accessible within the curly braces where it is defined. JavaScript is a dynamically typed language, meaning you don't need to specify data types. The typeof operator returns the data type of a variable. Arithmetic operators include addition, subtraction, multiplication, division, and modulus. The modulus operator returns the remainder of a division. String concatenation is done using the plus operator to join two strings together. Template literals use backticks and allow you to embed expressions using dollar sign curly braces. Template literals are much cleaner than string concatenation for building dynamic strings. Comparison operators include equal to, not equal to, greater than, less than. The double equals checks value equality, while triple equals checks both value and type. Always use triple equals for comparison in JavaScript to avoid unexpected type coercion. Logical operators include AND, OR, and NOT, written as double ampersand, double pipe, and exclamation mark. AND returns true only if both operands are true. OR returns true if at least one is true. NOT reverses the boolean value, turning true to false and false to true. The assignment operator is the equals sign. There are also compound assignment operators. Plus equals adds and assigns, minus equals subtracts and assigns, and so on. The increment operator plus plus increases a value by one. Decrement minus minus decreases by one. Type conversion can be done using Number(), String(), and Boolean() functions. JavaScript also performs automatic type coercion in certain situations which can be confusing. Understanding these fundamentals is crucial before moving to more advanced JavaScript topics.
